bucket: MTD for ITSA + Penalties & enquiries + Penalties, enquiries & HMRC nudge letters + Let Property Campaign & HMRC voluntary disclosure + Self-assessment mechanics
notes:
Both A19 + A20 picks written as full published blog pages. Per-page checks at write time: 0 em-dashes (Grep-verified each file), 0 Tailwind/className strings, metaTitle <= 62 chars (A19 51 / A20 54), metaDescription <= 158 chars (A19 146 / A20 146), 14 FAQs per page in YAML frontmatter array, HTML body (semantic

,

,
    ,
      , only). A19 anonymised personas per brief (James and Priya Whitwell for Form 17 60-day window walk; Marcus and Eleanor Sheridan for asymmetric-scope operational; Tom Whitaker and Olivia Bennett for tenants-in-common variant; David and Anna Marchand for gross-not-net split; Patricia and Henry Goodfellow for digital-exclusion overlay). A20 anonymised personas per brief (Alistair Brennan for points-threshold walk; Margaret Holloway for late-payment accelerated cascade; Eleanor Whitfield for digital-exclusion exemption; David Hartwell for income-exemption exit; Charles and Eleanor Hartfield for asymmetric-scope spouse coordination; Michael Cartwright for Perrin reasonable-excuse walk). Statutory citations verbatim from brief authority lists (A19: ITA 2007 s.836(2) default 50/50 + s.836(3) Exceptions A-F + s.837 Form 17 joint declaration with 60-day window and prospective-only effect; SI 2026/336 regs 18/20/24/25/27; TMA 1970 Sch A1 para 14(2) digital-exclusion definition; Hadee Engineering v HMRC [2020] UKFTT 0497 (TC) documentary discipline; Sokoya living-together limb cited at conceptual level rather than full SCD reference per brief noting reference but cautioning citation specificity. A20: FA 2021 Sch 24 paras 5(9)(b) 4-point threshold + 8(1) dual-condition reset + 8(4)(b) 12-month compliance + 15(2) point per failure + 15(4) £200 at threshold + 17 special circumstances + 22 reasonable excuse; FA 2021 Sch 26 as amended by Spring Statement 2025 with 15/30/31-day triggers and 3%/3%/10% percentages per §19.7 lock; SI 2026/336 regs 18/19/20/24/25/27; TMA 1970 Sch A1 para 14(2); TMA 1970 ss.31/31A/49; Perrin v HMRC [2018] UKUT 156 (TCC) four-stage framework; Martland v HMRC [2018] UKUT 178 (TCC) three-stage late-appeal; FA 2009 ss.101-102 interest cross-reference). Anti-templating maintained: A19 opens with share-of-gross + Form 17 asymmetry framing (not generic MTD threshold), Example 1 threshold-test matrix as semantic
      is the page's first concrete artefact per §19.4 anchor, Form 17 60-day window walked as
        , asymmetric-scope operational mechanics as prose + nested
          , gross-not-net split point and digital-exclusion overlay each walked as worked example; cross-link discipline bidirectional with A18 (qualifying-income deep-dive UPSTREAM) + A20 (penalty + exemption DOWNSTREAM for digital-exclusion + asymmetric-scope) + A7 (income-exit deep-dive). A20 opens with three-regime + comprehensive-exemption catalogue + operational what-to-watch framing per differentiator, Example 1 penalty + exemption matrix as semantic
      , late submission points-threshold walked as
        with arithmetic, late payment accelerated cascade walked as
          with arithmetic delineating MTD-15/30/31-3%/3%/10% from legacy-31/46/91-2%/2%/4%, digital-exclusion application walked with multi-ground evidence discipline, income-exemption-exit walked with cumulative-three-year test + post-2028/29 timing constraint, asymmetric-scope spouse coordination cross-linked to A19, Perrin four-stage framework walked with variations on facts, operational what-to-watch list with six discrete scenarios. F-3 drift catch on SI 2021/1076 → SI 2026/336 migration consistently navigated across both pages: both pages cite SI 2026/336 as the operative instrument from 1 April 2026 and call out SI 2021/1076 as the revoked prior instrument in the misconceptions list. F-6-style FA 2025 enacted-text Bill-vs-Act drift watchpoint navigated: A20 cites the Spring Statement 2025 acceleration consistently at the FA 2021 Sch 26 as-amended level with the verbatim 15/30/31-day 3%/3%/10% figures locked in per §19.7. Misframings GREP discipline: A19 13-pattern do-not-write GREP, A20 15-pattern do-not-write GREP, both pages include explicit Common Misconceptions sections enumerating the misframes with verdict; sessions writing on this content cannot inadvertently re-introduce the drift. Letting-agent net-of-fees trap (§19.13 Wave 4 lock) called out on both pages where joint-portfolio bookkeeping is in scope. Per-spouse ASA authorisation discipline (§19.10 Wave 4 lock) emphasised on A19 (primary), referenced on A20 (asymmetric-scope coordination context). No build step run in worktree (manager runs npm build after merge to main). No tracker / monitored_pages updates from worktree (manager handles main-side flips per pickup doc). No site-wide flags raised this batch beyond F-3 already logged.
